# Generate pin table for SPI link (2025-09-06 22:46Z)

_**User (2025-09-06 18:03Z)**_

print me the pin table for SPI inter-mcu link based on @pin_config.h 

---

_**Assistant (default)**_

I'll extract the SPI inter-MCU link pin configuration from the pin_config.h file and present it in a clear table format.

**Model**: Claude 3.5 Sonnet (2024-06-20), Large Language Model, Updated 2024-06-20

Based on the pin_config.h file, here's the SPI inter-MCU link pin table:

## SPI Inter-MCU Communication Pin Configuration

| **Signal** | **ESP32-S3 Pin** | **Daisy Seed Pin** | **Direction** | **Description** |
|------------|------------------|-------------------|---------------|-----------------|
| **SCLK** | GPIO 8 | D8 (SPI1_SCK) | ESP32 ← Daisy | SPI Clock (Daisy master) |
| **MOSI** | GPIO 5 | D10 (SPI1_MOSI) | ESP32 ← Daisy | Data from Daisy to ESP32 |
| **MISO** | GPIO 7 | D9 (SPI1_MISO) | ESP32 → Daisy | Data from ESP32 to Daisy |
| **CS** | GPIO 50 | D7 (SPI1_NSS) | ESP32 ← Daisy | Chip Select (Daisy master) |
| **IRQ** | GPIO 27 | - | ESP32 ← Daisy | Daisy IRQ line to ESP (active low) |
| **ATTN** | GPIO 41 | - | ESP32 → Daisy | ESP Attention output to Daisy (active high) |

## SPI Configuration Parameters

| **Parameter** | **ESP32 Value** | **Daisy Value** | **Description** |
|---------------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|
| **Host/Peripheral** | SPI3_HOST (slave) | SPI1 (master) | SPI controller assignment |
| **Clock Speed** | 10 MHz | 10 MHz | Clock frequency (master controlled) |
| **Mode** | Slave | Master | Communication mode |
| **Queue Size** | 8 | - | ESP32 SPI queue size |
| **DMA Channel** | SPI_DMA_CH_AUTO | - | ESP32 DMA channel |

## Ring Buffer Configuration

| **Buffer Type** | **Size** | **Description** |
|-----------------|----------|-----------------|
| **RX Ring** | 32 | Receive buffer size |
| **TX Ring** | 32 | Transmit buffer size |
| **Pool** | 16 | Memory pool size |

The configuration shows a master-slave SPI setup where the Daisy Seed acts as the SPI master and the ESP32-S3 acts as the SPI slave, with additional IRQ and attention lines for signaling between the two MCUs.
